import re

def test_inspect_prunes_hidden_and_dunder_dirs_in_os_walk__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/src/datasets/inspect.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert '__pycache__' not in source, "inspect.py should no longer hardcode pruning only '__pycache__' directories; it should prune all hidden ('.*') and dunder ('__*') directories."

def test_inspect_prunes_hidden_and_dunder_dirs_in_os_walk__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/src/datasets/inspect.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    pattern = 'dirnames\\[:\\]\\s*=\\s*\\[dirname\\s+for\\s+dirname\\s+in\\s+dirnames\\s+if\\s+not\\s+dirname\\.startswith\\(\\(\\s*\\"\\.\\",\\s*\\"__\\"\\s*\\)\\)\\s*\\]'
    matches = re.findall(pattern, source)
    assert len(matches) == 2, f'Expected both inspect_dataset and inspect_metric to prune os.walk by setting dirnames[:] = [dirname for dirname in dirnames if not dirname.startswith((".", "__"))]. Found {len(matches)} occurrence(s).'

# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_hidden_dir_pruning_uses_inplace_slice_assignment_and_no_dirnames_copy_enumeration__a1():
    source_path = '/workspace/src/datasets/download/streaming_download_manager.py'
    source = open(source_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'dirnames[:] = [dirname for dirname in dirnames if not dirname.startswith((".", "__"))]' in source, 'Expected hidden-directory pruning to be implemented via in-place slice assignment:\n    dirnames[:] = [dirname for dirname in dirnames if not dirname.startswith((".", "__"))]\nThis ensures os.walk pruning works by mutating the dirnames list in-place.'

def test_hidden_dir_pruning_uses_inplace_slice_assignment_and_no_dirnames_copy_enumeration__a2():
    source_path = '/workspace/src/datasets/download/streaming_download_manager.py'
    source = open(source_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'for i, dirname in enumerate(dirnames[:]):' not in source, 'Did not expect iteration over a shallow copy of dirnames (dirnames[:]) in enumerate(). The fix should avoid the confusing enumerate(dirnames[:]) + del pattern.'

def test_hidden_dir_pruning_uses_inplace_slice_assignment_and_no_dirnames_copy_enumeration__a3():
    source_path = '/workspace/src/datasets/download/streaming_download_manager.py'
    source = open(source_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert '[:] for the in-place list modification required by os.walk' in source, 'Expected an explanatory comment clarifying why [:] is used for in-place modification when pruning hidden directories during os.walk.'

def test_hidden_dir_pruning_uses_inplace_slice_assignment_and_no_dirnames_copy_enumeration__a4():
    source_path = '/workspace/src/datasets/download/streaming_download_manager.py'
    source = open(source_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'del dirnames[' not in source, "Did not expect deletion-based pruning (e.g., 'del dirnames[i]') to remain in the implementation."

import re

def test_inspect_prunes_hidden_and_dunder_dirs_in_os_walk__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/src/datasets/inspect.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'startswith((".", "__"))' in source, "Expected inspect.py to prune hidden/dunder directories using dirname.startswith(('.', '__'))"

def test_inspect_prunes_hidden_and_dunder_dirs_in_os_walk__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/src/datasets/inspect.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'dirname == "__pycache__"' not in source, 'Expected inspect.py to no longer special-case only "__pycache__" when pruning os.walk dirnames'

def test_inspect_prunes_hidden_and_dunder_dirs_in_os_walk__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/src/datasets/inspect.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert re.search('dirnames\\s*\\[:\\]\\s*=\\s*\\[', source), "Expected inspect.py to modify os.walk's dirnames in-place (dirnames[:] = ...), to ensure pruning actually affects traversal"
